hooks.command: { command -v graphify || python3 -c 'import graphify'; } >/dev/null 2>&1 || { command -v pip3 >/dev/null 2>&1 && nohup pip3 install --user --quiet graphifyy >/dev/null 2>&1 & } ; true

CMD=$(python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('tool_input',d).get('command',''))" 2>/dev/null || true); case "$CMD" in *grep*|*rg\ *|*ripgrep*|*find\ *|*fd\ *|*ack\ *|*ag\ *)   [ -f graphify-out/graph.json ] && { command -v graphify || [ -x "$HOME/.local/bin/graphify" ] || python3 -c 'import graphify' 2>/dev/null; } >/dev/null 2>&1 &&   echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","additionalContext":"MANDATORY: graphify-out/graph.json exists. You MUST run `graphify query \"<question>\"` before grepping raw files. Only grep after graphify has oriented you, or to modify/debug specific lines."}}'   || true ;; esac

HIT=$(python3 -c "import json,sys;d=json.load(sys.stdin);t=d.get('tool_input',d);exts=('.py','.js','.ts','.tsx','.jsx','.astro','.vue','.svelte','.go','.rs','.java','.rb','.c','.h','.cpp','.hpp','.cc','.cs','.kt','.swift','.php','.scala','.lua','.sh');vals=[str(t.get('file_path') or ''),str(t.get('pattern') or ''),str(t.get('path') or '')];j=' '.join(vals).lower().replace(chr(92),'/');tails=[('.'+x.rsplit('.',1)[-1]) for v in vals if v for x in [v.lower().replace(chr(92),'/').rsplit('/',1)[-1]] if '.' in x];sys.stdout.write('1' if 'graphify-out/' not in j and any(tl in exts for tl in tails) else '')" 2>/dev/null || true); if [ "$HIT" = 1 ] && [ -f graphify-out/graph.json ] && { command -v graphify || [ -x "$HOME/.local/bin/graphify" ] || python3 -c 'import graphify' 2>/dev/null; } >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","additionalContext":"MANDATORY: graphify-out/graph.json exists. You MUST run graphify before reading source files. Use: `graphify query \"<question>\"` (scoped subgraph), `graphify explain \"<concept>\"`, or `graphify path \"<A>\" \"<B>\"`. Only read raw files after graphify has oriented you, or to modify/debug specific lines. This rule applies to subagents too — include it in every subagent prompt involving code exploration."}}'; fi || true
